seoTitle: How to Send Emails to Customers in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Send account statements and direct messages to clients from inside QuickBooks Online without leaving your accounting workspace.
intro: Reaching out to clients about open balances doesn't have to mean switching to a separate email client and losing track of the conversation. This guide covers sending customer statements and direct messages right from a client's profile in QuickBooks Online, plus how to verify delivery. It's for business owners who want their client communication tied to their accounting records.

steps.heading: Open the customer's profile
steps.body: Go to Sales, then Customers, and click on the specific client you want to reach out to.

steps.heading: Start a new statement or message
steps.body: In the customer's transaction hub, click the New Transaction or Statement dropdown to begin drafting your communication.

steps.heading: Choose Statement for a balance summary
steps.body: Select Statement to compile a clear breakdown of the client's open ledger history into one document.

steps.heading: Customize the message and confirm the email
steps.body: Edit the greeting and body text, and double-check the client's email address is populated correctly.

steps.heading: Send and verify delivery
steps.body: Click Save and Send, then check the customer's activity timeline to confirm the email was successfully dispatched.

faq.question: Can I automate reminders for overdue invoices?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks lets you turn on auto-reminders for clients with overdue balances directly from the customer's action panel.

faq.question: Where do I edit the default email templates?
faq.answer: Global email subjects, bodies, and signatures can be customized in your company settings.

faq.question: How do I know if a client actually received the email?
faq.answer: Check the customer's activity history feed, which logs exactly when an email was sent from their profile.

faq.question: Can I send a message without attaching a statement?
faq.answer: Yes, you can send a general email directly from the customer's transaction hub without generating a formal statement document.
